newsapi.description: Oil edges higher as Mideast tensions continue
newsapi.content:
A Monday rally in chip stocks lost steam as AI-bubble anxieties and Mideast tensions continued to weigh on markets.

newsapi.title: New Boeing aircraft development hampered by backlog of existing orders, says CEO - Fox Business
newsapi.description: The aerospace giant is evaluating a possible new airplane design but says it must first improve its finances, technology and aircraft deliveries.
newsapi.content:
Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg said the global aerospace company has begun early work on a possible new airplane design but is not yet ready to move forward.

bbc.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
bbc.description: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

bbc.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
bbc.description: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

bbc.title: Carney looking at 'all options' as Trump announces 50% tariffs on Canada
bbc.description: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

bbc.title: Dozens hurt in Bologna protests after man dies while being restrained by police
bbc.description: Thousands of people took to the streets demanding justice for a Moroccan-born man who died on Sunday.

bbc.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
bbc.description: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

bbc.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill 12, including family of six, medics say
bbc.description: Firas al-Masri, his wife and their four children died in one attack, which the Israeli military says targeted a Hamas operative.

bbc.title: Two French firefighters die tackling blaze near Bordeaux airport
bbc.description: The blaze close to the parking lots at Bordeaux-Mérignac Airport is said to have originated from a brush fire.

bbc.title: Brazil's Amazon sees sharp decline in size of area burned in 2025
bbc.description: The amount of land burned in the world's largest rainforest was the smallest since MapBiomas records began in 1985.

bbc.title: Will AI help you do your job or replace you?
bbc.description: Artificial Intelligence companies make vast claims about their tools replacing human labour - our charts show what's happening.

bbc.title: VAT to be cut from household electricity bills in October
bbc.description: Homes will save around £45 a year through the cut announced on Andy Burnham's first full day as prime minister.

bbc.title: Ozempic-maker accuses rival of false advertising
bbc.description: Novo Nordisk has launched legal action accusing Eli Lilly of creating "misleading" ads, a claim its rival denies.

bbc.title: Thames Water lenders offer 'golden share' to head off nationalisation
bbc.description: The water supplier's lenders are trying to prevent the company from being taken over by the Burnham government

bbc.title: Lindt's Easter chocolate sales fall after price hike
bbc.description: The Swiss chocolate maker adjusts its strategy in response to weaker demand after its price rises.

bbc.title: Ryanair broken window may have been caused by 'foreign object'
bbc.description: Passenger Ljubisa Karović was partially sucked out of a cabin window on the Boeing aircraft.

bbc.title: UK borrows less than expected in June but public finances remain a challenge
bbc.description: Public finances for last month were better than expected but the UK is carrying major debt.

bbc.title: Three reasons why UK electricity prices are so high
bbc.description: The government has announced it will cut VAT on domestic electricity to zero but Britons still pay more for power than many other European nations.
